title: Facebook Messenger Customer Chat Integration
author: UCC INGO Dev Team
description: Integrate the Facebook Messenger Customer Chat Plugin into the UCC INGO website so visitors can directly chat with the UCC BSCS Facebook page from the browser without leaving the site.
background: The UCC INGO website currently has an AI chatbot (CSBot powered by Google Gemini) and social media links to the Facebook page (https://www.facebook.com/UCCBSCS2022). However, visitors who want to message the page directly must leave the site and open Facebook Messenger separately. This integration adds a persistent Messenger chat bubble on the website, enabling real-time conversations between visitors and the CS Council's Facebook page admins.
goals: Allow website visitors to chat directly to the UCC BSCS Facebook page from the website
Keep visitors on the site while enabling Messenger communication
Provide a familiar Facebook Messenger experience (blue chat bubble, Messenger UI)
Allow CMS admins to toggle the feature on/off without code changes
Ensure the widget does not conflict with the existing CSBot AI chatbot
non_goals: Building a custom chat system (using Facebook's official plugin instead)
Replacing the existing AI chatbot (CSBot) — both coexist
Automated bot responses via Facebook Send/Receive API (future enhancement)

how_it_works: Load the Facebook JS SDK asynchronously on page load
Initialize with the Facebook Page ID and App ID
The SDK auto-renders a Messenger chat bubble (bottom-right corner)
Clicking the bubble opens a Messenger chat window embedded in-page
Visitors can send messages directly to the Facebook page inbox
Page admins receive messages in their Facebook Page inbox / Messenger

prerequisites.facebook_page_id: REQUIRED — Numeric Page ID from the UCC BSCS Facebook page (found in Page Settings > General > Page ID)
prerequisites.facebook_app_id: RECOMMENDED — App ID from Meta Developers (https://developers.facebook.com) — Required for SDK initialization. Create a 'Consumer' type app if you don't have one.
prerequisites.messenger_plugin_enabled: In Facebook Page Settings > Messenger Platform > Whitelisted Domains, add the website domain (e.g., uccingo.tech)

implementation_slices.title: Environment Variables & Config
implementation_slices.description: Add FB_PAGE_ID and FB_APP_ID to .env file and expose them via next.config.mjs
implementation_slices.commit_message: feat(messenger): add Facebook env vars and Next.js config

implementation_slices.title: Facebook Messenger Component
implementation_slices.description: Create the core FacebookMessenger React component that loads the FB SDK, initializes the Customer Chat Plugin, and handles cleanup on unmount
implementation_slices.commit_message: feat(messenger): create FacebookMessenger component with SDK loader

implementation_slices.title: Component Styling
implementation_slices.description: Create CSS module for positioning, z-index management, and responsive tweaks to ensure the Messenger bubble doesn't overlap the CSBot widget
implementation_slices.commit_message: style(messenger): add positioning and z-index styles for Messenger widget

implementation_slices.title: Integration into _app.js
implementation_slices.description: Import and render FacebookMessenger in the AppInner layout, respecting siteConfig toggles and ensuring proper z-index stacking with CSBot
implementation_slices.commit_message: feat(messenger): integrate FacebookMessenger into app layout

implementation_slices.title: CMS Schema for Facebook Settings
implementation_slices.description: Add a 'Facebook Messenger' fieldset to siteConfig schema with fields for pageId, appId, greeting text, color, and enabled toggle — so admins can manage the feature from the CMS
implementation_slices.commit_message: feat(messenger): add Facebook Messenger fields to CMS siteConfig schema

implementation_slices.title: CMS-Driven Configuration
implementation_slices.description: Update FacebookMessenger component to read configuration from siteConfig (via Prefetcher) instead of hardcoded env vars, with env vars as fallback
implementation_slices.commit_message: feat(messenger): read Messenger config from CMS siteConfig with env fallback

implementation_slices.title: Z-Index & Coexistence Polish
implementation_slices.description: Fine-tune z-index stacking so both CSBot and Messenger bubbles are visible, adjust Messenger position on mobile to avoid overlap, add loading/error states
implementation_slices.commit_message: fix(messenger): resolve z-index conflicts and mobile positioning with CSBot

z_index_strategy.description: Both chat widgets live in the bottom-right corner. They must coexist without overlapping.

stacking.messenger_bubble: z-index: 999 (Facebook SDK default — higher than CSBot)
stacking.messenger_chat_window: z-index: 1001 (Facebook SDK default — opens above everything)

z_index_strategy.mobile_adjustment: On screens < 768px, Messenger bubble shifts to bottom-left to avoid overlapping the CSBot FAB

facebook_page_setup_checklist: 3. Under 'Whitelisted Domains', add: uccingo.tech (and localhost:3000 for dev)
5. Copy the Page ID from Settings > General > Page ID
6. Create a Meta Developer App at https://developers.facebook.com if no App ID exists
7. Add the Facebook SDK snippet to verify the app is connected

local: Messenger bubble appears in bottom-right (bottom-left on mobile)
Clicking bubble opens Messenger chat window
Can type and send a message to the Facebook page
CSBot still works independently in bottom-right
No console errors related to FB SDK
staging: Add Vercel domain to Facebook whitelisted domains
Full end-to-end test: send message → appears in FB page inbox
production: Add uccingo.tech to Facebook whitelisted domains
Verify Messenger bubble loads on live site
Test on Chrome, Safari, Firefox, mobile browsers

risks.mitigation: Graceful degradation — component renders nothing if SDK fails to load
SDK is loaded asynchronously (async/defer) and only initializes after DOM ready
CSS positioning shifts Messenger to bottom-left on mobile; z-index management ensures both are accessible
Widget only works after domain is added in Facebook Page Settings > Messenger Platform

future_enhancements: Facebook Send/Receive API for automated bot replies
Custom greeting text that changes based on page context (e.g., thesis page vs blog)
Analytics tracking — count Messenger opens and messages sent
Integration with the existing CSBot — CSBot can hand off to Messenger for human support
